title: HTML Best Practices Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of the essential rules for writing clean and professional HTML code.

questions.question: According to industry standards, what is the best case for writing tag and attribute names?
options: UPPERCASE
lowercase
CamelCase
Sentence case

questions.question: Is it required to quote attribute values in HTML5 if the value has no spaces?
options: Yes, always.
No, it's never required.
Only for image sources.
It depends on the browser.

questions.question: Why should you avoid using inline styles (style='...') in your HTML?
options: They make the page load much slower.
They make it difficult to maintain and update the design across multiple pages.
They are not supported by modern browsers.
They cause security vulnerabilities.

questions.question: What is the result of using an empty alt attribute (alt='') for an image?
options: The image will not load at all.
Screen readers will skip the image as decorative.
The browser will show a placeholder.
The page will fail the accessibility audit.

questions.question: Which of these is the most accurate description of 'separation of concerns' in web development?
options: Using as many different frameworks as possible.
Keeping HTML for structure, CSS for style, and JavaScript for logic.
Developing the mobile version before the desktop version.
Using separate servers for images and text.
